reason: This entry appears to be a data collision between two different '4DS' tickers: Daldrup & Söhne AG (German company, XETRA) and 4DS Memory Ltd (Australian company, ASX). The country, country_code, and ISIN all point to an Australian entity while the name and exchange point to a German one. The '4ds memory' alias confirms cross-company contamination. Recommend dropping this entry until the two records are cleanly separated and each assigned correct metadata.

This entry is deeply inconsistent: the name is Assicurazioni Generali S.p.A. (an Italian insurer), but the country is Australia, the ISIN has an AU prefix, and several aliases (ashante gold, aurora spine, autosports) point to entirely different companies that trade as ASG on Australian or other exchanges. The ISIN AU000000ASG2 almost certainly belongs to an Australian company (possibly Autosports Group or similar), not to Generali. Without external evidence, the entry cannot be corrected reliably and should be dropped or rebuilt from scratch with the correct source record.

This entry appears to conflate two or three unrelated instruments: the name references a Direxion leveraged AVGO ETF (US/NASDAQ), but the ISIN, country, and aliases all point to Australian or Canadian securities. The metadata is internally contradictory and no external evidence is available to resolve it. Prefer drop_entry; if the intent was to represent the Direxion AVL product, the entire metadata set needs reconstruction from scratch.

This entry is a leveraged inverse ETF wrapper ('Tradr 2X Short Be Daily ETF') — a synthetic/derivative instrument that should not be in a plain equity/stock universe. All four aliases belong to unrelated issuers and must be removed. Additionally, the combination of BATS exchange with Australian country/ISIN is internally inconsistent and cannot be validated without external evidence. Recommend drop_entry as a leveraged daily ETF wrapper; at minimum flag for human review due to metadata contradictions.

The entry appears to be a structured trust instrument (CORTS = Corporate-Backed Trust Securities, GIC = Guaranteed Investment Contract) linked to Aon Capital, not a standard equity. It should not be in a stock universe. Both aliases ('kontron', 'kootenay silver') are entirely unrelated to this instrument and belong to separate companies. The AT country code and ISIN are inconsistent with a NYSE-listed US trust. Recommend drop_entry.

This entry is internally contradictory: the name and aliases point to PG&E Corp (a US NYSE-listed utility) while the exchange is NYSE but country, country_code, and ISIN all point to Australia. The alias 'pengana capital' suggests this may actually be Pengana Capital Group (ASX:PCG), an Australian fund manager with ticker PCG on the ASX. The record appears to be a corrupt merge of two unrelated PCG tickers. Recommend dropping and re-ingesting as two separate, correctly attributed entries after human verification.
